summary: Iteration 16 — verified all 3 follow-ups: (1) client-side Classic/New layout switch button with localStorage persistence, (2) global masked-username Latest Orders feed on new dashboard, (3) sender-side read-receipt flip via GET /messages/thread since=... including {from_id:me, read_at>since}. All 4 backend tests pass; UI dual-flow via Playwright confirmed msg-sent → msg-read flip within ~2.4s after recipient reads.

issues: 7 of 11 seeded orders render as '###' because their username field is empty/null in DB (mask fallback). Not a regression but noise — consider filtering out empty-username orders from /api/orders/latest-global.

action_items: (Optional) Filter out empty/null usernames from /api/orders/latest-global so the feed only shows meaningful masked entries.
(Optional) Silence 'Each child in a list should have a unique "key" prop' React warning seen on new dashboard render.
critical_code_review_comments: ClientDashboard.jsx is 900+ lines — consider splitting NewHomeView, ClassicHomeView, and Header components into separate files for maintainability.
GET /orders/latest-global has no rate limiting despite being a PUBLIC endpoint; consider a light IP-based cap to avoid scraping.
seed_data_creation: No new seed data. Test sends transient DMs 'flip-<ts>' between testbugfix1 and Balkin — harmless.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Layout switch button uses data-testid='switch-layout-btn' (green) and 'switch-layout-btn-classic' (blue). Poller in MessagesView is 2s. Admin toggle key is 'use_new_home_layout' (not 'use_new_layout'). Login requires captcha; parse via GET /api/auth/captcha then submit with captcha_id + captcha_answer. Frontend routes: no /client/messages; use /client/dashboard then click [data-testid=nav-messages] (classic layout) — new layout uses in-page state changeView('messages'). For UI read-receipt flip, keep layout on classic so nav-messages is directly clickable.
